prompt: Tight macro detail of a contractor's calloused hands holding a steel 25-foot tape measure pulled taut across a freshly installed ceiling beam, measuring the spacing for a coffer grid. The hands show real wear — a small cut on one knuckle, sawdust under the fingernails, a leather watch band on one wrist. A pencil tucked behind the ear partially in frame. Fine sawdust drifts visibly through the air, caught mid-fall in a beam of light. Shot with a 100mm macro lens, very shallow depth of field — the steel tape blade and the closer hand tack-sharp, the beam softly defocused. A single warm tungsten work-lamp from the upper right creates dramatic directional light, deep falloff into shadow on the left. Slightly desaturated palette, the brand-red lock button on the tape measure is the only saturated color. NOT HGTV bright, working-contractor catalog feel. No readable text, no logos.
